title: luzula racemosa var. humilis
intro: Luzula racemosa var. humilis, also known as dwarf woodrush, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to mountainous regions, characterized by its dense tufts of grass-like leaves and short spikes of tiny white flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponin and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Luzula racemosa var. humilis are said to include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and aid in the treatment of respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used in potpourri or as a natural dye.
